tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

Currently, two types of queries are supported:

  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.

    "text": "cyberdyne bob",

</example>

## DXOS — The Decentralized Operating System

### Overview

<cite index="1-3">DXOS is an open source framework for building real-time, collaborative web applications that run entirely on the client and communicate peer-to-peer, without the need for centralized servers.</cite> Often referred to as the "Decentralized Operating System," <cite index="3-1">DXOS is a developer of a decentralized platform designed to create the first functionally and commercially competitive, fully decentralized alternative to the corporate cloud.</cite>

### Company Details

- **Founded:** <cite index="3-5">DXOS was founded in 2021.</cite>
- **Headquarters:** <cite index="3-6">DXOS is headquartered in New York, NY.</cite>
- **Status:** <cite index="3-8">DXOS is a Private company.</cite>
- **Industry:** <cite index="3-7">DXOS's primary industry is Software Development Applications.</cite>
- **Community:** <cite index="31-7">The community spans 8 cities worldwide, with 30+ contributors and 200+ community members.</cite>
- **Team:** <cite index="6-6">DXOS was created by a small core team of developers from many countries.</cite>
- **Investors:** <cite index="3-10">Backed VC, BlueYard Capital, Empty Set Group, and PL Capital (United States) have invested in DXOS.</cite>

### Mission

<cite index="8-3">DXOS — The decentralized operating system — is an independent public organization whose mission is to provide open source technologies that allow for a free and open decentralized internet.</cite> <cite index="8-6,8-7">The DXOS platform consists of decentralized protocols, tools, and applications that provide an alternative to the current Web. DXOS enables the development of privacy-preserving applications for its users, and a fairer marketplace for open source developers.</cite>

### Core Technology Stack

DXOS is built around three primary protocols/components:

1. **ECHO (Data):** <cite index="1-6">ECHO is an open source database architecture that incorporates transparent data replication and conflict resolution for secure and scalable local storage.</cite>

2. **HALO (Identity):** <cite index="1-7">HALO seamlessly manages digital identities using public/private key pairs allowing nuanced control over access to applications, databases, and networked devices.</cite>

3. **MESH (Network):** <cite index="1-9">MESH extends existing internet protocols to enable secure and resilient peer-to-peer networks without the need for centralized server infrastructure.</cite>

### Technical Details

- **Language:** <cite index="4-4">DXOS provides a TypeScript implementation of the DXOS protocols, SDK, toolchain and Composer.</cite>
- **CRDT Support:** <cite index="1-11,1-12">DXOS leverages Automerge — a powerful CRDT that enables multiple concurrent users to operate on the same data without loss or conflict.</cite>
- **Networking:** <cite index="1-19">It automatically synchronizes data across networked devices using WebRTC for cloudless peer-to-peer networking.</cite>
- **Architecture:** <cite index="1-14,1-15">The only reason you need a central server is to serve your application shell. Everything else happens in the client and works peer-to-peer.</cite>
- **APIs:** <cite index="5-5,5-6">DXOS works in any Node.js or Browser environment. There is a TypeScript API and a React API.</cite>
- **Reactive UI:** <cite index="1-20">It re-renders UI components automatically as the underlying data changes, with minimal impact on performance.</cite>

### Composer — Flagship Application

<cite index="37-1">Composer is a collaborative productivity app where developers can build and organize knowledge, extend with custom data and UI, and run private AI against their knowledge locally.</cite> <cite index="10-6">Composer is a local-first workspace that is designed for small team collaboration.</cite>

Key Composer features include:
- **Documents:** <cite index="31-17">Realtime collaboration inside of a modern markdown editor.</cite>
- **Sheets:** <cite index="35-12,35-13">Supports 300+ basic calculations along with support for custom functions. Extendable via API calls and custom script integrations.</cite>
- **Sketching:** <cite index="36-2">The Sketch plugin allows you to leverage a digital whiteboard and diagram editor powered by TLDraw.</cite>
- **Plugins:** <cite index="31-39,31-40">Everything inside of Composer is a plugin. This means you can leverage core functionality or build out whatever you need.</cite>
- **AI Integration:** <cite index="31-29">Leverage machine learning to build agentic workflows that enhance team productivity while protecting your intellectual property.</cite>
- **Data Ownership:** <cite index="31-1,31-2">You own everything inside of Composer. No spyware, no algorithms, no lock-in.</cite>
- **Import/Export:** <cite index="31-51">Import and export your entire database as plain, human readable markdown, JSON, and media files.</cite>
- **Agents:** <cite index="9-2">DXOS is launching a fully-managed service to host agents.</cite>

### Developer Experience

<cite index="6-12">DXOS wraps its three main components together in a set of SDKs (currently, React and TypeScript) that enable developers to deliver sophisticated applications quickly.</cite>

The SDK includes:
- A `@dxos/client` npm package for TypeScript
- A `@dxos/react-client` package with React hooks
- Chrome DevTools extension for debugging
- Vite as the recommended bundler
- Project templates and configuration examples

### Organizational Structure

<cite index="8-4,8-5">DXOS.org is a decentralized global community of open source developers. The community is organized via an independent foundation (DXOS.foundation), which is funded by members of the community.</cite>

The foundation organizes efforts around three teams:
- **DXOS Protocols:** <cite index="8-15">Development and support of decentralized protocols that initially include: HALO (identity), ECHO (data), and MESH (network).</cite>
- **DXOS Platform:** <cite index="8-17,8-18">Development and operation of a permissionless blockchain (DXNS) and the peer-to-peer KUBE network that provides the operational platform for DXOS applications.</cite>
- **Application Showcase:** Building flagship apps like Composer to demonstrate platform capabilities.
